races.name: Tokushima Marathon 2026
races.location: Tokushima City and several other prefectures. (Tokushima) , Japan
races.description: This marathon takes you along the clear waters of the Yoshino River—known as the "Shikoku Saburō"—with scenic views of Tokushima City from Mt. Bizan watching over you. The course is relatively flat, and the generous 7-hour time limit makes it friendly for all runners!  This will be the final opportunity to run on the current course. Whether you're taking on the Tokushima Marathon for the first time or have joined us many times before, come be a part of this memorable "last run" on the current course!

info.Location: Tokushima City and several other prefectures. (Tokushima) , Japan
info.Organizers: Tokushima Marathon Planning Committee

Event/Eligibility.Marathon(42.195km)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\n\t: (1)Male and Female age 19 or older. (2) Those who are expected to finish by the race cut-off time Time Limit：7 Hours *Cutoff points exist.

info.Participation Fee: Marathon(42.195km) :11,000 JPY (optional participation gift available for an additional 2,000JPY).For Challenge & Charity participants, contributions start from 1,000JPY per unit
info.Check-in Time: Saturday, March 21, 2026 | 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM ※Race day check-in is not available.
info.Participant’s Prize: Tokushima Marathon original T-shirt (optional) [Finisher's Prize] Tokushima Marathon original finisher's medal, Tokushima Marathon original finisher's towel
info.Award Ceremony: Up to 10th place each male and female overall. Up to 1st place each in General Male and Female. Up to 3rd place each male and female in all age groups in increments of 5 years.
info.Transportation Facility: Short walk from Awa-Tomida Station.
info.Other: Free shuttle bus available from temporary parking lot.

notice: Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.
Each person is limited to one event registration.

There will be no race guide email distributed by the organizer. Please check the event website and social media for updates as information will be posted there. If the organizer deems it necessary, emails may be sent.
Wheelchairs are not permitted. If you have a disability and find it difficult to run alone, please arrange for an escort runner to accompany you. ※(Guide dogs are not permitted.)

races.name: The 16th Shibuya-Omotesando Women's Run
races.description: A women-only 10km race through the streets of Shibuya and Omotesando. Debut runners are welcome. Why not run your first race in "Shibuya-Omotesando"?

info.Organizers: Shibuya-Omotesando Women's Run Executive Committee

Event/Eligibility.10km: Female: Ages 16 or older Time Limit：1 Hour 30 Minutes（from 9:00AM *Start time）; there are three cutoff points.

info.Participation Fee: 10km: 8000 JPY
info.Check-in Time: On the race day from 7:00AM
info.Check-in Location: Athlete bibs and participant's prize will be available at the general information desk at the venue on the event day.
info.Award Ceremony: 1st to 6th overall, 1st to 6th of each age category, Up 1st to 6th Place Debut Runners
info.Transportation Facility: 15-minute walk from Shibuya Station on JR/Tokyu Line, 10-minute walk from JR Harajuku Station.

notice: Please note that entries will close once the maximum capacity has been reached.
Only one application per person is allowed for this event. If multiple applications by the same person are found, the applicant will be disqualified.
Group entry is not available.
The participation information is expected to be sent by email in early March .

races.name: 2026 Kikuchi Sakura Marathon
races.description: The event is held to run along the roads of "Kikuchi, the Healing Village," which heralds the beginning of cherry blossom blooming, and to promote health and interaction through having fun while running, thereby contributing to the creation of a lively and energetic community.

info.Organizers: Kikuchi City, Kikuchi City Board of Education

Event/Eligibility.Half Marathon (21.0975km): Male Age 19 - 39, 40 or older and Female Age 19 or older Time Limit：2 hours 30 minutes
Event/Eligibility.10km: Male Age 16 - 39, 40 or older and Female Age 16 or older

info.Participation Fee: Age 19 or older : 4,800 JPY Age 7 - 18 : 2,000 JPY ※Insurance included
info.Check-in Time: On the day of the race from 8:00AM to 9:00AM
info.Check-in Location: Kikuchi City Hometown Creation Civic Square at event headquarters inside the venue.
info.Start Line: In front of the Kikuchi Regional Development Bureau, Kumamoto Prefecture
info.Award Ceremony: 1st to 3rd place in each division ※In the 2km race, there will be a Fighting Spirit Award for men and women aged 60 and over.
info.Transportation Facility: Approximately 1 hour by Kumamoto Dentetsu bus from JR Kumamoto Station, get off at Kikuchi Civic Square. Approximately 20 minutes from Ueki IC on the Kyushu Expressway.
info.For Inquiries: Kikuchi Sakura Marathon Executive Committee Secretariat

notice: Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.
The Race Guide will be posted on the event website and sent via email approximately two weeks before the tournament (early March).

races.name: Tokushima Marathon 2026 Challenge Run
races.location: Tokushima City and several other prefectures. (Tokushima) , Japan
races.description: A "Challenge" for Future Marathon Runners! We're once again holding the popular Challenge Run, open to participants from  age 7 and up. As with last time, there will be two categories: 1.5 km and 3 km, and best of all—participation is completely free! Since the run starts from the same location as the Tokushima Marathon, you'll get to experience the real marathon atmosphere. Perfect for beginner runners—don't miss it!

info.Location: Tokushima City and several other prefectures. (Tokushima) , Japan
info.Organizers: Tokushima Marathon Planning Committee

Event/Eligibility.Challenge Run (3km): Age 13 or older Time Limit：until 10:15 AM, 1 cut-off point
Event/Eligibility.Challenge Run (1.5km): Age 7 or older (Age 7-9 & wheelchair users must be with parent age 18 or older) Time Limit：until 10:15 AM, 1 cut-off point

info.Participation Fee: Challenge Run 3km & 1.5km : Free. (optional participation gift available for an additional 2,000 JPY).
info.Check-in Time: Saturday, March 21, 2026 | 10:00 AM – 6:00 PM ※Race day check-in is not available.
info.Award Ceremony: There will be no awards ceremony or record measurement.
info.Capacity: Challenge Run (3km) : 200 people Challenge Run (1.5km) : 400 people ※ (including 30 overseas players of each categories)
info.Transportation Facility: Short walk from Awa-Tomida Station.

notice: Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.
Each person is limited to one event registration.

There will be no race guide email distributed by the organizer. Please check the event website and social media for updates as information will be posted there. If the organizer deems it necessary, emails may be sent.

races.name: 18th HASETSUNE 30K
races.description: The Hasetsune 30K is a hard sport that takes place in the natural surroundings of Okutama. There are no aid stations along the way. Starting in 2023, the course will be more aggressive and include a higher percentage of trails, making it a full-fledged short trail running race. As with the Hasetsune Cup, we hope you will challenge your limits.

info.Organizers: Mountain Sport Association of Japan

Event/Eligibility.Approx. 30km [General]: Male and Female aged 16 or older Time Limit：7 hours and 15 minutes. check points exist.※Wave Start
Event/Eligibility.Approx. 30km [Elite]: Male and Female aged 16 or older Time Limit：7 hours and 15 minutes. check points exist.※Wave Start

info.Participation Fee: Age 18 or older :12,000 JPY Age 16~17 : 6,000 JPY ※For members of Japan Trail Running Association or Tokyo Mountaineering Federation, the fee is reduced by 1,000 JPY
info.Check-in Time: The day before, March 28th (Sat) 1:00PM-4:30PM The day of the event, March 29th (Sun) 6:00AM-8:00AM
info.Participant’s Prize: Original event shirt featuring Edo vegetable "Norabona"
info.Award Ceremony: Top 6 overall for both men and women and top 6 for each age group
info.Transportation Facility: Akigawa Keikoku Rivertio is located a 25 minute walk from JR Musashi-Itsukaichi Station
info.Other: A parking lot will be available within walking distance of the venue (※Reservations are scheduled to begin from February onwards.)

notice: Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.

Participation information from the tournament office will be notified by email approximately 2 weeks before the race. We will ask for your email address to receive an email informing you of your participation in the tournament at the time of application.
The Elite Division is open to the following participants. Please confirm your eligibility before applying.
・Must meet the General Division requirements plus one of the following conditions:
・Past winners of this event
・Athletes with an ITRA Performance Index total score of 600 or higher for men, or 560 or higher for women, as of December 2025

The events you can apply for differ depending on whether you are a 2025 Registered Athlete of the Japan Trail Running Association (Trail Run JAPAN) or a member of the Tokyo Metropolitan Mountaineering Federation (participation fee discount applies). Please take care when selecting your event.
A portion of the participation fee will be used for environmental conservation activities in Okutama.

races.name: 18th Shimanto River Sakura Marathon
races.description: Run alongside the majestic Shimanto River, known as one of Japan’s Three Great Clear Streams, and experience the warm hospitality and cheers from the local community as you make your way to the finish line. Along the route, vibrant sakura in full bloom will greet you, making your race an unforgettable journey!

info.Organizers: Shimanto River Sakura Marathon Organizing Committee

Event/Eligibility.42.195km: Men: 18–29 years, 30–49 years, 50 years and above, Women: 18–49 years, 50 years and above Time Limit：6 hours ※ With a cutoff point (25 km mark: 3 hours 30 minutes from the start)

info.Check-in Time: 【Day Before】 Saturday, March 28 – 1:00 PM to 5:00 PM 【Race Day】 Sunday, March 29 – 6:30 AM to 8:20 AM
info.Check-in Address: 7-8 Kotohira-machi, Shimanto-cho, Takaoka-gun, Kochi Prefecture, Japan
info.Finish Line: Showa Furusato Exchange Center, Shimanto Town – Shuttle bus service available to the start point
info.Participant’s Prize: Original towel, local products, tote bag
info.Award Ceremony: 6th place overall for men and women, 3rd place in each category, and other special awards.
info.Transportation Facility: A short walk (10 minutes) from JR Dosan Line Kubokawa Station. By car, about 10 minutes from Shimanto Town Chuo IC.
info.Other: Parking is available at various locations around the start area. For details, please refer to the official race website.

notice: - Once your BIB Exchange Voucher (Participation Ticket) is ready, a notification will be sent to the email address registered with your RUNNET account.
Please download your participation ticket from the “Participation Ticket Issuance Notice” sent by email, and either save it on your smartphone or print the PDF to bring with you to the venue.
- Baggage (excluding valuables) can be checked at the start/check in area. The organizers will transport it to the finish line.
≪Course Information≫
Start: Kubokawa Elementary School → Goal: Furusato Exchange Center

- Some sections of the course are very narrow. Moving along the course to cheer runners (by family, friends, or supporters) is strictly prohibited, as it can interfere with race operations and compromise runner safety.
Officials will check vehicle numbers, and any runner whose supporters are moving along the course may be disqualified.
From this year, these measures have been strengthened. The organizers are not responsible for any accidents caused by moving support.
- Most of the course runs alongside regular traffic. Please be careful and avoid running side by side, in large groups, or overtaking suddenly in a way that obstructs vehicles.

races.name: FUKUI SAKURA MARATHON 2026
races.description: Experience the heart of Fukui like never before at the Fukui Sakura Marathon! Run through breathtaking cherry blossom spots, alongside the sleek Hokuriku Shinkansen, and along a course inspired by the shape of a dinosaur – a truly unique route that lets you feel all of Fukui in one unforgettable race.  Savor the flavors of local cuisine, soak up the warm cheers from spectators, and embrace the vibrant spring atmosphere. Under our inspiring slogan, “Your Passion Blossoms, Sakura Blooms,” let your running spirit flourish and make this spring in Fukui a memory to last a lifetime!

info.Organizers: Fukui Sakura Marathon Executive Committee, Fukui Prefecture, Fukui City, Sakai City, Fukui Athletics Association (General Incorporated Foundation), Fukui Shimbun

Event/Eligibility.Marathon―42.195KM\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\n\t\: Participants must be 18 years of age or older Time Limit：Total of 7 hours｜Official End Time: 3:30 PM, incorporating eight cutoff points

info.Check-in Time: March 28 ※Day Before Race Day｜10 AM–6 PM ※Tentative.
info.Participant’s Prize: Original t-shirt｜Finisher's Prizes: Medal and Towel
info.Award Ceremony: Overall Awards―Men & Women: Top 8 finishers (gross time). Ceremony for top 3; 4th–8th place certificates mailed afterward.｜Age Group Awards―Men & Women: Top 8 in each age group―29 and under, 30s, 40s, 50s, 60s, 70 and over (net time). No ceremony; certificates mailed (Japan addresses only). ※Ages are as of race day. Overall winners are excluded from age group awards.
info.For Inquiries: Fukui Sakura Marathon Executive Committee Office

notice: Please review the information below and read the full "Event Guidelines" and "Application Guidelines" on the official website before applying.
1. Registration for the event may close prior to the published deadline should participant capacity be reached.
2. Please note that for registrants in Turkey, payment via credit card or PayPal is not available.
3. In early March, the event organizers will distribute the official race guide and athlete bib vouchers electronically via email to all registered participants.
Check-in will occur exclusively on the day preceding the event. Check-in on the day of the event is not available.
① Required Documentation: All participants must present their passport for identification. Proof of foreign residency is also required.
Please be advised that proxy pick-up is strictly prohibited; each participant must check in personally.

② Distributed Items: Upon successful check-in, participants will receive the following: an athlete bib, a transponder timing chip, a participation gift, a baggage check bag, and any other applicable items.
To facilitate a secure and organized commencement, the event will employ a staggered start time system divided into two waves: Wave 1 will begin at 8:30 AM, and Wave 2 will begin at 8:40 AM.

races.name: 26th HITACHI SAKURA ROAD RACE
races.description: The Hitachi Sakura Festival is being held at the time, so it's a lively event with many tourists cheering on. There is also a course that runs under a cherry blossom arch and along the seaside.

info.Organizers: Hitachi Sakura Road Race Executive Committee, Hitachi City, Hitachi City Board of Education, Ibaraki Shimbun, Hitachi City Athletics Association, Hitachi Amateur Sports Association (Public Interest Foundation)

Event/Eligibility.Half (21.0975km): Male Ages 16-39, 40s, 50s, 60 or older, Female Ages 16-39, 40 or older Time Limit：Target time 2 hours 25 minutes*Check points exist.
Event/Eligibility.10km: Male Ages 16-39, 40s, 50s, 60 or older, Female Ages 16-39, 40 or older Time Limit：Target time 1 hour 20 minutes *Check points exist.
Event/Eligibility.5km: Male Ages 16-39, 40s, 50s, 60 or older, Female Ages 16-39, 40s, 50 or older Time Limit：Target time 1 hour *Check points exist.
Event/Eligibility.Family (1.8km): Participants can be 2-5 family members or friends. At least one person under the age of 15 must participate. ※There are no prizes.
Event/Eligibility.Relaxing cherry blossom viewing (1.8km): Male and Female, Ages 16 or older ※There are no prizes.

info.Participation Fee: Half, 10km, 5km: 4,000JPY. Ages 13-15,1.8km: 1,000JPY. Family(2 person group), Relaxing cherry blossom viewing 2,500JPY. Family(3 person group): 3,000JPY. Family(4 person group): 3,500JPY. Family(5 person group): 4,000JPY
info.Check-in Time: 7:30 AM April 5
info.Check-in Location: Runners support corner at the venue
info.Opening Ceremony: 8:10AM ※Participation in the opening ceremony is optional.
info.Start Line: Heiwa-dori Ave ※"The street extending west from the central exit of JR Hitachi Station"
info.Finish Line: Near Hitachi Civic Center New Town Square
info.Award Ceremony: Top 10 in each category ※On the day, the first winners of each category will be awarded on stage. 2nd to 10th place in each category will be awarded at the "Awards for 2nd and Below" area.
info.Transportation Facility: 2 minutes walk from the central exit of Hitachi Station on the JR Joban Line
info.For Inquiries: Hitachi Sakura Road Race Executive Committee Secretariat

notice: -Please note that entries will be closed once maximum capacity has been reached.
-The Race Guide will be sent via email in late March.

races.name: 28th Ome Takamizu Palatinose Int'l Trail Run
races.description: This race, which began in 1999, is a pioneering domestic trail running competition. Since then, it has become established as a race that signals the start of the domestic trail running season. The race aims for Takamizuyama's "Jofukuin Temple" and Ome's hidden village, "Kurihira Village." The race venue is an 8-minute walk from JR Ome Station. A trail running festival is also planned. Four participants will be invited to the 30km division of the Saipan Marathon 2027.

info.Organizers: Ome Takamizu International Trail Run Executive Committee

Event/Eligibility.[Advanced class] 30km (GPS 24.5km): Male and Female Aged 16 or older Time Limit：5 Hours (1 hour 50 minutes at 10km point)
Event/Eligibility.[Intermediate class] 15km (GPS 14.3km): Male and Female Aged 16 or older Time Limit：None

info.Participation Fee: 30km : 9000 JPY 15km : 8000 JPY
info.Check-in Time: On the race day, April 5th 7:30AM
info.Participant’s Prize: Japanese tenugui (hand towels) designed by wrought iron craftsman Nishida Mitsuo and Coca-Cola
info.Award Ceremony: Overall 1st to 6th place for both men and women, 1st to 3rd place for both men and women by age group *Award certificates for each age group will be sent via email in PDF format.
info.Transportation Facility: 8 minutes walk from Ome Station on the JR Ome Line

notice: Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.
The Race Guide will be sent via email two weeks prior to the event.
[Required Items]
1000ml or more of drinking water, gloves, and health insurance card. ※Trail running shoes are recommended.

The advanced class aid station is located at the entrance to Omotesando after passing Takamizusan Jofukuji Temple. The intermediate class aid station is located at the end of Kurihira Forest Road. Please dispose of empty bottles at the designated location.

races.name: The 4th Ayabe Suigen no Sato Trail Run
races.description: Highlights of This Race: Experience the beauty of lush forests and crystal-clear rivers. Enjoy heartfelt aid stations offering delicious local specialties. The race venue is located right next to Ayabe Onsen! After the race, you can relax in the hot springs. We aim to create an race that runners, staff, and the local community can all enjoy!

info.Organizers: Ayabe Suigen no Sato Trail Run Executive Committee

Event/Eligibility.【50K】51.2km: Male & Female age 18 or older Time Limit：11 hours *Cut-off points available
Event/Eligibility.【15K】16.7km: Male & Female age 15 or older Time Limit：5 hours *Cut-off points available

info.Participation Fee: 【50K】: 16,500円 JPY * Student discount 8,250 JPY, 【15K】: 7,000 JPY * Student discount 3,500 JPY
info.Check-in Time: [April 5] 【50K】: 6:30AM～8:30AM, 【15K】: 9:00AM～10:30AM * No prior-day registration; same-day registration only.*Please submit a pledge form etc.
info.Check-in Location: Ayabe Onsen Special venue (Niou Park Ground Golf Course)
info.Check-in Address: 10 Ainomukae, Mutsuyoricho, Ayabe City, Kyoto Prefecture
info.Start Line: Ayabe Onsen Special Venue (Niou Park Ground Golf Course)
info.Finish Line: Ayabe Onsen Special Venue (Niou Park Ground Golf Course)
info.Award Ceremony: 【50K】Prize winners: The top 6 finishers in each gender. Age-group: Up to the top 3 in each gender. 【15K】Prize winners: The top 3 finishers in each gender. Age-group: Up to 1st place in each gender *Priority will be given to the winners, and there will be no overlap.
info.Transportation Facility: Access: By car—about 30 min from Ayabe IC (Maizuru-Wakasa Expwy) from Osaka, 30 min from Kyotanba Wachi IC (Kyoto Jukan Expwy) from Kyoto, and 30 min from Oi Takahama IC (Maizuru-Wakasa Expwy) from Tsuruga. From Route 27, turn right at Yamaya (Kyoto side, 20 min) or Uminpia (Obama side, 40 min). By train—get off at Ayabe Station (JR San’in or Maizuru Line).
info.Other: If you wish to use the parking lot, please be sure to apply at the time of entry.

notice: Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.
Please check
the official event website
and make an entry. Please translate and see.
【About Student Discounts】

●For the 50K, participants aged 18 (excluding high school) to 24 years old are eligible for student discount, and for the 15K, participants aged 15 to 24 years old are eligible for student discount. Eligible events (selection of entry button) vary depending on student status. Please be careful when selecting your event. *Students must submit a photo of their student ID card by email (info@fun-trails.com) at least 10 days prior to the race.

●Participation information from the race secretariat will be sent via email approximately 2 weeks before the race. Upon application, we will ask for your email address where you would like to receive such information.

races.name: FUJI HOKUROKU RELAY MARATHON
races.description: Enjoy the relay marathon at the foot of the majestic Mount Fuji, a World Heritage Site.

info.Organizers: Challenge Fuji Five Lakes Executive Committee

Event/Eligibility.Fun Run 1.5km (Kids): Kids age 9 - 12 years old who can complete the race within 25 minutes. Time Limit：25 Minutes
Event/Eligibility.Fun Run 1.5km (Family): Parent and child(age6-9)who can complete the race within 25 minutes. Only one parent(age18 or older) per boy or girl may participate. Time Limit：25 Minutes
Event/Eligibility.4-Hours Team: Representative age 18 or older(Team of 2-8 members) & others members age 12 or older※ Must include one manager (who may also serve as a player). Time Limit：4 Hours
Event/Eligibility.4-Hours Individual: Male & Female age 16 or older. Time Limit：4 Hours

info.Participation Fee: Fun Run 1,000 JPY 4-Hours Team(2-8members) 6,500 JPY per member 4-Hours Individual 7,000 JPY
info.Check-in Time: On the day (Fun Run (Kids) 9:45am-10:30am, Fun Run (Family) 9:45am-11:00am, 4-Hours Team/Individual 9:45am-11:30am)
info.Award Ceremony: 4-Hours Team: Top 3 teams 4-Hours Individual: Top 3 places *One award certificate will be given per winning team or individual.
info.Capacity: Fun Run (kids50 people, 50 families, 100 people) 4-Hours Team: 200 teams (2-8 members), 100 individuals
info.Transportation Facility: By car: Approximately 10 minutes from the Higashi Fuji Five Lakes Road [Fujiyoshida IC] Approximately 6 minutes from the Higashi Fuji Five Lakes Road [Fujiyoshida Oshino Smart IC] By train: Approximately 15 minutes by taxi from Fujikyuko Line [Mt. Fuji Station]
info.Other: Parking fee is 1,000 JPY. Parking permits are issued. Private parking available.
info.For Inquiries: Fuji Hokuroku Relay Marathon race Secretariat

notice: Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.
Please note that the race secretariat will not deliver a Number Card Exchange Voucher by postal mail.
Registration information from the race secretariat will be sent via email. When you register, you will be asked for an email address where you would like to receive event information emails.
Run Japan is unable to provide information or respond to inquiries regarding the refund guarantee program. For details, please contact the race office listed under Inquiry.
Applications for the Family Run must be made in the name of a parent or guardian (age 18 or older) who will be running.
Please be sure to read the details on the official website and understand them before entering.
■ Regarding Team Division Applications

- Teams consisting entirely of under 18 or younger are not permitted to participate. All team members must be 18 years of age or older . Please apply in the name of a runner 18 years of age or older .
- Only one team per representative application is permitted.
- One manager is required (may also serve as a runner). If the manager is not also a runner, there is no participation fee.
- Team member information will be provided when the race guide is released.

- Event changes are not permitted after application. Inquiries regarding additions to the number of participants and changes to team names can be made by contacting the race secretariat during the application period only. Please note that refunds will not be given for team reductions.

races.name: Tohoku Food Marathon 2026
races.description: A festive marathon for both runners and non-runners alike! Enjoy the famous local cuisine of the Tohoku region, savor the brewing water used for Japanese sake, and run along the beautiful lakeside in this fan run event in the coming spring. After crossing the finish line, indulge in a festival featuring specialty dishes from across Tohoku and delightful Japanese sake. Eat, drink, run, and have a great time experiencing the best of Tohoku!

info.Organizers: Tohoku Food Marathon 2026 Executive Committee

Event/Eligibility.Full Marathon（Start time 8:30）: Age 16~ *Male and female who are healthy and able to complete the race Time Limit：6hours 30minutes
Event/Eligibility.Half Marathon（Start time 9:00-9:30）: Age 16~ *Male and female who are healthy and able to complete the race Time Limit：3hours 30minutes
Event/Eligibility.Half Marathon（Start time 9:30-10:00）: Age 16~ *Male and female who are healthy and able to complete the race Time Limit：3hours 30minutes

Event/Eligibility.5km run: Age 13~ *Male and female who are healthy and able to complete the race Time Limit：1hour
Event/Eligibility.Parent-child run (500m): Parents over 18 years old and children under age12 Time Limit：20 minutes

info.Participation Fee: Marathon: 9,900 JPY, Half Marathon: 7,700 JPY, 5km 3300 JPY, Parent-child run: 1,100 JPY

info.Start Time: 8:30(Full Marathon（Start time 8:30）) 9:00(Half Marathon（Start time 9:00-9:30）) 9:30(Half Marathon（Start time 9:30-10:00）) 10:00(Half Marathon（Start time 10:00-10:30）) 10:30(Half Marathon（Start time 10:30-11:00）) 13:00(5km run) 11:30(Parent-child run (500m))

notice: -Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.
-The race guide will be sent by email by the organizer in early April.
-For overseas runners, you can pick up your bibs on the race day at the Help Desk located around the T-shirts pick-up areas of the main venue, Naganuma Futopia Park.

-The full marathon starts at 8:30, and the half marathon can start at any time within the 30-minute time slot you specified when registering between 9:00 and 11:00. The parent-child run starts at 11:30, so if you are participating in both the half marathon and parent-child run, please start the half marathon early.
-No changes are possible once you complete your registration.

races.name: 36th CHALLENGE FUJI 5 LAKES ULTRAMARATHON
races.description: Enjoy the Ultra Marathon to the fullest while taking in the views of the majestic Mount Fuji, a World Heritage Site, the beautiful lake, and rows of cherry blossom trees in full bloom.

info.Organizers: The Challenge Fuji 5 Lakes Executive Committee

Event/Eligibility.FUJI 5LAKES 120km: [General] Male/Female, aged 18 or older， [U25] Male/Female, aged 18-25 *Wave start Time Limit：15 hours 15 minutes *Cutoff at Check Points included.
Event/Eligibility.FUJI 4LAKES 100km: [General] Male/Female, aged 18 or older， [U25] Male/Female, aged 18-25 *Wave start Time Limit：14 hours *Cutoff at Check Points included.

races.name: 2026 Aomori Sakura Marathon
races.description: Run Through Aomori’s Scenic and Cultural Highlights!  Start at Nogikawa Park, famous for its cherry blossoms, and finish at Aoi-Umi Park, a beautiful waterfront near Aomori Station, close to attractions like Nebuta House Wa Rasse and A-FACTORY. The course is mostly flat, with a highlight at the Aomori Bay Bridge, featuring a 20-meter elevation change and breathtaking views of Mutsu Bay. Aomori also offers rich cultural experiences – visit historic sites like Sannai-Maruyama and Komakino before or after the race, and enjoy sightseeing in this vibrant city. Experience the beauty and culture of Aomori in a marathon you’ll never forget!

info.Organizers: Aomori City, Aomori Athletics Association, Aomori Sakura Marathon Executive Committee

races.name: International Blind Marathon 2026
races.description: The International Blind Marathon is held concurrently! The event features a scenic course set around Japan's second-largest lake, Lake Kasumigaura, and the warm hospitality of local volunteers cheering along the route. Known for its unique charm distinct from urban marathons, this event is a favorite among runners.

info.Organizers: Kasumigaura Marathon Organizing Committee, Tsuchiura City, Kasumigaura City, Japan Blind Marathon Association, Japan Para Sports Association / Japanese Paralympic Committee, Ibaraki Athletics Association, Tsuchiura City Council of Social Welfare, Kasumigaura City Council of Social Welfare, The Mainichi shin-bun, Sports Nippon shin-bun

Event/Eligibility.Full Marathon(42.195km)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\: By gender, age 16 or older, for visually impaired persons or IPC-registered individuals. Time Limit：6 hours (Until 12:15 for 17 km, until 14:10 for 28.5 km)
Event/Eligibility.10-mile(approx.16.09km)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\: By gender, age 16 or older, for visually impaired persons. Time Limit：2 hours
Event/Eligibility.5km: By gender, age 16 or older, for visually impaired persons. Time Limit：40 minutes

info.Participation Fee: Full Marathon(42.195km) 12,000 JPY, 10-mile(approx.16.09km) 9,000 JPY, 5km 6,000JPY
info.Check-in Time: 19th April, 7:30am - 9:00am
info.Check-in Location: Information Desk in the "B" Sub Baseball Field (next to Kawaguchi Athletic Park)
info.Finish Line: Kawaguchi Athletic Park Athletics Stadium (J:COM Field Tsuchiura)
info.Participant’s Prize: 1. One of the following: an original race shoulder pouch or a local specialty. *Participation prize for 1. will be given out at the main venue on the day. 2. "Bishoku Rice" made with 100% local Koshihikari brown rice. [Finisher's Prize] 100-year-old wooden barrel-brewed soy sauce "Shiho Freshness Preservation Bottle 200ml"
info.Award Ceremony: General Division and IPC Registered Division: Top 3 finishers in each category (men and women), outstanding athletes receive the Minister of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Award; top finishers' escort runners receive the Outstanding escort runners Award (B1, B2)
info.Transportation Facility: 5 minutes on foot from JR Tsuchiura Station East Exit. 15 minutes by car from Sakuratsuchiura IC or Tsuchiura-Kita IC on the Joban Expressway.
info.Other: A free shuttle bus runs from the temporary parking lot.

notice: - Each participant may register for only one category.

-200JPY of the tournament entry fee will be used to help with Lake Kasumigaura Water Purification Fund and as a subsidy for guide dog training.

races.name: Mt.FUJI100 2026【Charity Entry】
races.description: This is a World Trail Majors race and features Japan’s largest 100-mile trail race, attracting top runners from Japan and around the world. The race will be held in three categories: FUJI100mi, KAI70k, and ASUMI40k. All races finish at Fuji Hokuroku Park.

info.Organizers: Mt.FUJI100 Executive Committee

Event/Eligibility.【April 25】 KAI70k: Any male or female: Age 18 years or older Time Limit：Until April 26 11:00AM
Event/Eligibility.【April 25】ASUMI40k: Any male or female: Age 18 years or older Time Limit：Until April 25 9:30PM

Please note that the donation is not eligible for tax deductions. Cancellations or refunds cannot be accepted once the entry is completed. Please submit your entry acknowledging the above.

races.name: Sado Toki Marathon 2026
races.description: This marathon takes place on Sado Island, a remote island rich in nature where people and the internationally protected Japanese Crested Ibis coexist. The course is well known for its magnificent nature, including the blue ocean and snow-capped mountains, and the T-shirts are always a hit!

info.Organizers: Sado Toki Marathon Executive Committee

- No Performance Index score is required for SAKUYA.

races.name: 2026 UEDA VERTICAL RACE - The 12th Mt. Taro Mountain Climbing Race -
races.description: Japan's first urban mountain running race. The Sky Running Vertical and Sky Race will be held on Mt. Taro, beloved by the citizens of Ueda.  This year, the Ueda Sky Race (27 km in distance / 3100 m of cumulative elevation gain) will be held on May 4 as the 7 race of the Skyrunner World Series.

You will be asked to indicate your preference during the registration process, and if you have an accompanying runner, you will be asked to enter their name and relationship to the parent or guardian.

races.name: The 5th FunTrails Round Minano 50K/30K
races.description: This course will take you to the scenic spots of Minano Town by connecting trails. We will also offer great views from the ridge tops and delicious local products at the aid stations. Please enjoy the course!

info.Organizers: FTR MINANO Executive Committee

Event/Eligibility.Minano 50K（51km）: Male and Female: Age 18 or older Time Limit：12 hours, there are some cutoff points
Event/Eligibility.Minano 30K: Male and Female: Age 16 or older Time Limit：8 hours, there are some cutoff points

info.Participation Fee: Minano 50K 16,500 JPY（Student discount 8,250 JPY aged 18-24）, Minano 30K 9,900 JPY（Student discount 4,950 JPY aged 16-24）*For student discount, students must submit a photo of their student ID by email.
info.Check-in Time: May 9 (Sat.) ［Minano 50K, 30K］1:00 PM-5:00 PM, May 10(Sun.),［Minano 50K］5:00 AM-6:30 AM,［Minano 30K］7:30 AM-9:00 AM
info.Check-in Address: Shimodano, Minano Town, Chichibu District, Saitama Prefecture
info.Award Ceremony: Minano 50K: Top 6 runners in each division, Top 1 runner in each age group; Minano 30K: Top 3 runners in each division, Top 1 runner in each age group. *Priority will be given to the overall winner and there will be no overlap with the age group winner.
info.Transportation Facility: 【By Train】1.8km from Kami Nagatoro Sta., 1.4km from Oyahana Sta. 【By Car】Hanazono I.C., Kanetsu Expressway, approx. 15.9 km Honjo Kodama I.C., Kanetsu Expressway, approx. 24.3 km
info.Other: 50K: Minano Sports Park Multipurpose Field (350 cars). 30K: Minano Town Hall (100 cars) & Town Sports Park (200 cars). Shuttle buses run from 7:00 AM.
info.For Inquiries: FTR MINANO Executive Committee (FunTrails LLC Secretariat)

notice: ●Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.

●Registration information for the tournament will be sent by email from the tournament secretariat approximately 3 weeks before the race and also be posted in the NEWS section of the event websites.. When you register, you will be asked for an email address where you would like to receive tournament information emails.

●For those who are 24 years old or younger, the events they can participate in vary depending on their student status. Please be careful when selecting your event. *If you are registering in the student division, please submit a photo of your student ID card by email (info@fun-trails.com) at least one week prior to the race. If we are unable to verify your student ID, you may be denied entry.
●Luggage storage available (excluding valuables). Please bring your own bag (maximum 90L).
●About parking lot
50K: Minano Sports Park Multipurpose Field 350 cars / 30K: Minano Town Hall 100 cars, Minano Town Sports Park 200 cars
※Shuttle service to the event venue available from 7:00 AM.

races.name: THE13TH RUNNET EKIDEN OKINAWA
races.description: This Ekiden Race, runners can run alongside the beach with refreshing feeling. A flat and easy-running course will welcome everyone to join!  Don’t be so hurry to go home soon after the race because the beach has a BBQ area.  It must be super fun to have a beachside party after finishing the race!

info.Organizers: General Incorporated Association R-bies Sports Foundation

Event/Eligibility.Solo 18km: Male and Female: Age 18 or older

info.Participation Fee: 【Solo】5,500JPY 【EKIDEN Walk 3km】1,000JPY
info.Participant’s Prize: 【Solo】T-shirt or a pair of socks 【EKIDEN Walk 3km】drinks
info.Award Ceremony: Top 3 finishers of Male and Female （+ other special awards) *No awards for "EKIDEN Walk 3km."
info.Transportation Facility: By Car, Bus or Taxi

notice: - Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.
- The Race Guide will be sent via email about 2 weeks before the race day.

- The course for this event is not reserved for participants (runners). Local cyclists and pedestrians may be sharing the same course during the race. Please be sure to pay great attention to running safely and give consideration to others.
- We set limit number of participants to ensure the safety of the course.
- Running alongside the participants by bicycle is prohibited for safety reasons.
-Wheelchair is not allowed in this race.
-【Solo】3 km × 6 laps
-If participants are under 18 years old, guardian's consent is required.

races.name: 46th Kanuma Satsuki Marathon
races.description: This marathon event takes place in Kanuma City, where the Kanto Plain meets the Nikko Mountain Range. The course features a mix of urban streets and countryside landscapes, offering runners a scenic view of the Nikko Mountains in the distance. At the finish line, participants can enjoy strawberries, a specialty of Kanuma, known as the "City of Strawberries." Experience the charm of early summer in Kanuma through this marathon.

info.Organizers: Kanuma City, Kanuma City Board of Education, Kanuma City Sports Association, Organized by: Kanuma Satsuki Marathon Executive Committee

Event/Eligibility.Half marathon: Male: Age 15-39, 40-49, 50-59, 60-69, 70 and above. Female: 15-34, 35-50, 51 and above. Time Limit：180 min. *Cutoff points exist.
Event/Eligibility.10km: Male: Age 15-39, 40-49, 50-59, 60-69, 70 and above. Female: 15-34, 35-50, 51 and above. Time Limit：85 min.*Cutoff points exist.
Event/Eligibility.5km: Male: Age 15-39, 40-49, 50-59, 60-69, 70 and above. Female: 15-34, 35-50, 51 and above. Time Limit：45 min.
Event/Eligibility.3km: Male and Female: Age 12-14, Jogging 15 and above. Time Limit：30 min.
Event/Eligibility.2km: Age 6–8, 9–11, and parents or guardians Age 18 and above. Time Limit：20 min.

info.Participation Fee: Half marathon 4500 JPY, 10km and 5km 4000 JPY, 3km 2000 JPY, 2km 2,500 JPY per group, Age 15-17 2000 JPY, Age 12-14 1500 JPY
info.Check-in Time: Around 7:00AM - 8:00AM
info.Check-in Location: Kanuma City Gotenyama Park Baseball Stadium
info.Award Ceremony: Top 5 in each category (excluding the 3km general men's and women's events and the 2km) *Results will be based on gross time.
info.Capacity: Half marathon 2200 people, 10km 1800 people, 5km 1500 people, 3km 500 people, 2km 1000 pair
info.Transportation Facility: It is a 15-minute walk from Shinkanuma Station on the Tobu Nikko Line, a 20-minute walk from Kanuma Station on the JR Nikko Line, and a free shuttle bus from each station. It is a 5-minute walk from Tenjincho on the Kanto Bus Utsunomiya Line. It is a 20-minute drive from Kanuma IC on the Tohoku Expressway.
info.Other: Parking is available only to residents outside Kanuma City. Spaces are limited, so please use transit. Kanuma City residents cannot use this lot.

notice: Please note that the entry will be closed once the maximum capacity has been reached.
The 2km race for ages 6 to 8 starts at 9:40AM.

Please register for the 2km race under the name of the participating child age 6 - 11. Additionally, to ensure the safety of participating children, it is mandatory for a guardian or parent to also participate in the race.
During the registration process, you will be asked to confirm whether to purchase a race bib clip. Please check the race bib clip design on the event's official website.
The race guide will be sent by the organizer via email. It is expected to be sent around late April.

During the registration process, you will be asked to confirm Use of Parking Lot. We will be sending parking passes via email to those who wish to use the parking lot. Please print it out and bring it with you.
* This information is current at the time of race registration. Please check the e-mail from the organizer for the final information.
If you are a disabled person and require an escort runner, the participation fee for the escort runner will be waived (up to one person). Please note that the escort runner will not be timed.
